theorem intervalIntervalIntegrable_iff_intervalIntegrable_smul
    {E : Type*} [NormedAddCommGroup E]
    {a b : ℝ} {μ : MeasureTheory.Measure ℝ}
    {R : Type*} [NormedAddCommGroup R] [SMulZeroClass R E] [IsBoundedSMul R E]
    {f : ℝ → E} (r : R) :
    IntervalIntegrable f μ a b ↔ IntervalIntegrable (r • f) μ a b := by
  have h₁ : IntervalIntegrable f μ a b ↔ IntervalIntegrable (r • f) μ a b := by
    constructor
    · intro h
      -- Use the fact that the interval integral of (r • f) is r times the interval integral of f
      have h₂ : IntervalIntegrable (r • f) μ a b := by
        -- Apply the bounded scaling property to the interval integral
        have h₃ : IsBoundedSMul R E := inferInstance
        -- Use the fact that the interval integral of (r • f) is r times the interval integral of f
        exact IsBoundedSMul.intervalIntegrable_smul r h
      exact h₂
    · intro h
      -- Use the fact that the interval integral of f is (1/r) times the interval integral of (r • f)
      have h₂ : IntervalIntegrable f μ a b := by
        -- Apply the bounded scaling property to the interval integral
        have h₃ : IsBoundedSMul R E := inferInstance
        -- Use the fact that the interval integral of f is (1/r) times the interval integral of (r • f)
        have h₄ : IntervalIntegrable (r • f) μ a b := h
        -- Since R is a normed additive commutative group, we can find the inverse of r
        have h₅ : ∃ (s : R), s • (r • f) = f := by
          use (1 : R) / r
          simp [smul_smul]
          <;>
            (try norm_num) <;>
            (try simp_all [div_eq_mul_inv]) <;>
            (try ring_nf) <;>
            (try field_simp) <;>
            (try norm_num)
          <;>
            (try linarith)
        -- Use the fact that the interval integral of f is (1/r) times the interval integral of (r • f)
        cases' h₅ with s h₅
        have h₆ : IntervalIntegrable (s • (r • f)) μ a b := by
          have h₇ : IsBoundedSMul R E := inferInstance
          exact IsBoundedSMul.intervalIntegrable_smul s h₄
        have h₈ : s • (r • f) = f := h₅
        have h₉ : IntervalIntegrable f μ a b := by
          simpa [h₈] using h₆
        exact h₉
      exact h₂
  exact h₁

theorem intervalIntervalIntegrable_iff_intervalIntegrable_smul
    {E : Type*} [NormedAddCommGroup E]
    {a b : ℝ} {μ : MeasureTheory.Measure ℝ}
    {R : Type*} [NormedAddCommGroup R] [SMulZeroClass R E] [IsBoundedSMul R E]
    {f : ℝ → E} (r : R) :
    IntervalIntegrable f μ a b ↔ IntervalIntegrable (r • f) μ a b := by
  have h₁ : IntervalIntegrable f μ a b ↔ IntervalIntegrable (r • f) μ a b := by
    constructor
    · intro h
      -- Use the fact that the interval integral of (r • f) is r times the interval integral of f
      have h₂ : IntervalIntegrable (r • f) μ a b := by
        -- Apply the bounded scaling property to the interval integral
        have h₃ : IsBoundedSMul R E := inferInstance
        -- Use the fact that the interval integral of (r • f) is r times the interval integral of f
        exact IsBoundedSMul.intervalIntegrable_smul r h
      exact h₂
    · intro h
      -- Use the fact that the interval integral of f is (1/r) times the interval integral of (r • f)
      have h₂ : IntervalIntegrable f μ a b := by
        -- Apply the bounded scaling property to the interval integral
        have h₃ : IsBoundedSMul R E := inferInstance
        -- Use the fact that the interval integral of f is (1/r) times the interval integral of (r • f)
        have h₄ : IntervalIntegrable (r • f) μ a b := h
        -- Since R is a normed additive commutative group, we can find the inverse of r
        have h₅ : ∃ (s : R), s • (r • f) = f := by
          use (1 : R) / r
          simp [smul_smul]
          <;>
            (try norm_num) <;>
            (try simp_all [div_eq_mul_inv]) <;>
            (try ring_nf) <;>
            (try field_simp) <;>
            (try norm_num)
          <;>
            (try linarith)
        -- Use the fact that the interval integral of f is (1/r) times the interval integral of (r • f)
        cases' h₅ with s h₅
        have h₆ : IntervalIntegrable (s • (r • f)) μ a b := by
